messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
I heard somewhere that Mencius might have had a hand in writing Mozi — does that make sense?

That doesn't hold up. The Mozi (墨子) is attributed to Mozi, not Mencius. These are two entirely separate thinkers from distinct philosophical schools. Mencius was a Confucian — he developed and extended Confucian ethics, particularly around the innate goodness of human nature. Mozi, by contrast, founded the Mohist school, which was in many respects a rival to Confucianism. In fact, Mencius himself explicitly criticized Mohist ideas in his own writings. So not only did Mencius not write the Mozi, the two figures represent opposing philosophical camps.
